title: Implement beamr-cli — thin runner for Gate 1
purpose: The CLI is how we prove the VM works. Until Meridian embeds beamr in-process, the CLI is the only way to load a .beam file and run it. This brief delivers argument parsing, .beam file loading, the execution pipeline (load -> resolve -> execute -> report), and structured error output. It is the capstone of Gate 1 — if beamr-cli can load a Gleam .beam file, resolve its imports, execute a function, and print the result, Gate 1 passes.
task: Implement the beamr-cli binary in crates/beamr-cli/src/main.rs. The CLI accepts a .beam file path and an optional entry point (module:function/arity). It creates an AtomTable, registers Gate 1 BIFs, loads the .beam file via the loader, resolves imports, and executes the entry function. If no entry point is specified, it defaults to <module>:main/0. An 'imports' subcommand prints the unresolved import report for a .beam file without executing. All errors produce human-readable messages on stderr with appropriate exit codes. NOTE: B-009 scopes the CLI to loading and import reporting. Full end-to-end execution (load → spawn → interpret → exit) requires interpreter briefs (B-013+) and is validated at Gate integration time, not by this brief.

requirements.title: Argument parsing and validation
requirements.spec: beamr-cli SHALL accept the following invocations: (1) beamr <file.beam> [module:function/arity] to load and execute, (2) beamr imports <file.beam> to report imports, (3) beamr --help/-h for usage, (4) beamr --version/-V for version. The .beam file path SHALL be validated to end with .beam. The entry point SHALL be validated as module:function/arity format where arity is a valid u8. Unknown flags SHALL produce an error naming the flag.
acceptance: beamr hello.beam parses as a Run command with no explicit entry
beamr hello.beam hello:main/0 parses as a Run command with entry
beamr imports hello.beam parses as an Imports command
beamr --help parses as Help
beamr --version parses as Version
beamr hello.txt produces an error about non-.beam file
beamr hello.beam bad-entry produces an error about invalid entry format
beamr --unknown produces an error about unknown flag

requirements.title: Execution pipeline
requirements.spec: WHEN a .beam file is loaded for execution THE SYSTEM SHALL: (1) create an AtomTable with common atoms, (2) create a BifRegistryImpl and register Gate 1 BIFs, (3) load the .beam file via beamr::loader::load_module, (4) determine the entry point — if not specified, use <module_name>:main/0, (5) look up the entry function via the module registry, (6) create an initial process, (7) execute via the interpreter until the process terminates, (8) print the return value to stdout or the error to stderr. Exit code SHALL be 0 on success, 1 on runtime error, 2 on load error.
acceptance: Loading a valid .beam file with a main/0 function executes and prints the result
Loading a .beam file with unresolved non-BIF imports prints the unresolved report and exits 1
A runtime error (badarith, undef, etc.) prints the error to stderr and exits 1
A load error (invalid format, missing chunk) prints the error to stderr and exits 2
Exit code 0 on successful execution

requirements.title: Import report command
requirements.spec: WHEN beamr imports <file.beam> is invoked THE SYSTEM SHALL load the .beam file, run import resolution against the Gate 1 BIF registry (with no modules pre-loaded), and print the unresolved import report to stdout. Each unresolved import SHALL be printed as module:function/arity, one per line, grouped by module. The exit code SHALL be 0 regardless of whether imports are resolved — the report is informational.
acceptance: beamr imports hello.beam prints unresolved imports grouped by module
Each line shows module:function/arity format
Imports that match registered Gate 1 BIFs are NOT shown (they are resolved)
Exit code is 0 even if unresolved imports exist

requirements.title: Error display and exit codes
requirements.spec: ALL errors SHALL be printed to stderr with the prefix 'beamr: '. Load errors SHALL use the Display implementation from beamr::error::LoadError. Runtime errors SHALL use ExecError::Display. IO errors (file not found, permission denied) SHALL include the file path and OS error message. The CLI SHALL NOT panic on any input — all error paths return structured CliError values.
acceptance: File not found produces: beamr: cannot read 'missing.beam': No such file or directory
Invalid .beam format produces: beamr: load: invalid .beam format
Runtime badarith produces: beamr: exec: badarith
No panic on any malformed input (fuzz-test with garbage .beam bytes)

requirements.title: Unit tests for argument parsing
requirements.spec: WHEN the CLI is tested THE SYSTEM SHALL include tests for: all argument parsing paths (run, imports, help, version), entry point validation (valid and invalid formats), .beam path validation, error display formatting, and unknown flag handling. Tests SHALL live in a #[cfg(test)] mod tests block within main.rs.
acceptance: cargo test -p beamr-cli passes with all tests green
Tests cover: help flag, version flag, beam file only, beam file + entry, imports command, non-.beam rejection, invalid entry rejection, unknown flag rejection

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement interactive REPL mode — batch execution only
SHALL NOT implement multi-file loading from command line — load one .beam file (multi-module loading is the loader's job via imports)
SHALL NOT implement NIF registration from CLI flags — NIFs come from the host (Meridian), not the CLI
SHALL NOT implement any core VM logic — the CLI is a thin wrapper calling beamr crate APIs
SHALL NOT add CLI argument parsing libraries (clap, structopt) — the argument grammar is simple enough for manual parsing
SHALL NOT claim end-to-end bytecode execution — interpreter briefs (B-013+) are prerequisites for that; this brief validates load + report + basic spawn/exit
verification: cargo test -p beamr-cli passes with all tests green
beamr nonexistent.beam prints error to stderr and exits non-zero
Code inspection: main.rs has no unwrap() or expect() outside #[cfg(test)]
